ontology.foodon_gap_note: Roti John is a type of omelette sandwich; 'sandwich' (FOODON_00002497) is the closest parent class available. No specific FoodOn entry exists for Roti John.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Roti John. 'Sandwiches' (A0011) is the closest parent category available.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT does not have a specific code for Roti John. 'Sandwich (food)' (22620006) is the closest parent concept available.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Roti John Mutton is one piece, which weighs approximately 150 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore Foodo — Roti John Mutton
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data directly from HPB Singapore Foodo for 'Roti John Mutton'. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat were not explicitly reported by HPB; saturated fat was estimated from typical minced mutton and egg composition using USDA FoodData Central as a secondary reference. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown is not available in the HPB record.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — White Baguette (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Roti John Mutton. GI estimated at 65 based on the primary carbohydrate component, white baguette bread (GI 65–70 per University of Sydney GI Database). The protein and fat from egg and mutton may marginally lower the composite GI, but a medium category (55–69) is appropriate. Glycemic load calculated as (65 × 15.63g carbohydrate per 100g × 1.5 serving factor) / 100 ≈ 15.2 for a 150g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (314 mg/100g) with low potassium and fibre offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Roti John Mutton due to its sodium content of 314 mg/100g, which is compounded by soy sauce and condiment additions; requesting reduced sauces and avoiding mayonnaise is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate from high-GI white baguette with low dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roti John Mutton has moderate carbohydrate (15.6 g/100g) from a high-GI white baguette base and low fibre (0.7 g/100g); strict portion control and avoidance of sugary accompaniments such as sweet chilli sauce or kecap manis are essential for blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from mutton and egg combined with elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roti John Mutton contributes saturated fat (~2.1 g/100g) and cholesterol (34 mg/100g) from mutton and egg alongside high sodium (314 mg/100g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency and consider leaner protein substitutes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roti John Mutton is a beloved Singaporean and Malaysian street food consisting of a French baguette sliced lengthwise, dipped in a seasoned egg-and-minced-mutton mixture with chopped onions and spices, then pan-fried until the egg coating is golden and crispy while the bread interior remains soft. The dish delivers a savory, slightly spicy flavour profile with a contrast of textures — a crisp exterior and a tender, juicy filling. It is commonly served with chilli sauce, ketchup, or mayonnaise. Nutritionally, each 100g provides 141 kcal, 6.8g protein, 5.48g fat, and 15.63g carbohydrate with very low sugar (0.32g) and modest fibre (0.7g). Sodium is relatively high at 314 mg/100g, making it a concern for hypertensive individuals. The white baguette base carries a medium glycemic index (~65), requiring portion discipline for those managing type 2 diabetes. The mutton component contributes saturated fat and cholesterol relevant to cardiovascular health monitoring. Roti John is a culturally significant hawker staple in Singapore, commonly found at Malay food stalls and pasar malam night markets.
common_user_queries: How is Roti John Mutton made?
Is Roti John Mutton suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient data sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo for the exact dish 'Roti John Mutton', an approved primary source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on the primary carbohydrate component (white baguette). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central as a secondary reference for fatty acid composition of minced mutton and egg.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown and moisture data for Roti John Mutton are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient data for this specific dish preparation is not reported in approved sources as of 2026. Saturated fat (2.1 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference values for minced lamb and egg composition and documented as an adaptation.
